1.english: Ta, Seen, Meem.
1.transliteration: Taa-Seeen-Meeem

1.english: These are the verses of the clear Book.
1.transliteration: Tilka Aayaatul Kitaabil Mubeen

1.arabic: لعلك باخع نفسك ألا يكونوا مؤمنين
1.english: Perhaps, [O Muhammad], you would kill yourself with grief that they will not be believers.
1.transliteration: La'allaka baakhi'un nafsaka allaa yakoonoo mu'mineen

1.arabic: إن نشأ ننزل عليهم من السماء آية فظلت أعناقهم لها خاضعين
1.english: If We willed, We could send down to them from the sky a sign for which their necks would remain humbled.
1.transliteration: In nashaa nunazzil 'alaihim minas samaaa'i Aayatan fazallat a'naaquhum lahaa khaadi'een

1.arabic: وما يأتيهم من ذكر من الرحمن محدث إلا كانوا عنه معرضين
1.english: And no revelation comes to them anew from the Most Merciful except that they turn away from it.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yaateehim min zikrim minar Rahmaani muhdasin illaa kaanoo 'anhu mu'rideen

1.arabic: فقد كذبوا فسيأتيهم أنباء ما كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: For they have already denied, but there will come to them the news of that which they used to ridicule.
1.transliteration: Faqad kazzaboo fasa yaateehim ambaaa'u maa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: أولم يروا إلى الأرض كم أنبتنا فيها من كل زوج كريم
1.english: Did they not look at the earth - how much We have produced therein from every noble kind?
1.transliteration: Awa lam yaraw ilal ardi kam ambatnaa feehaa min kulli zawjin kareem

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inn fee zaalika la Aayah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.arabic: وإذ نادى ربك موسى أن ائت القوم الظالمين
1.english: And [mention] when your Lord called Moses, [saying], "Go to the wrongdoing people -
1.transliteration: Wa iz naadaa Rabbuka Moosaaa ani'-til qawmaz zaalimeen

1.english: The people of Pharaoh. Will they not fear Allah?"
1.transliteration: Qawma Fir'awn; alaa yattaqoon

1.arabic: قال رب إني أخاف أن يكذبون
1.english: He said, "My Lord, indeed I fear that they will deny me
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbi inneee akhaafu ai yukazziboon

1.arabic: ويضيق صدري ولا ينطلق لساني فأرسل إلى هارون
1.english: And that my breast will tighten and my tongue will not be fluent, so send for Aaron.
1.transliteration: Wa yadeequ sadree wa laa yantaliqu lisaanee fa arsil ilaa Haaroon

1.arabic: ولهم علي ذنب فأخاف أن يقتلون
1.english: And they have upon me a [claim due to] sin, so I fear that they will kill me."
1.transliteration: Wa lahum 'alaiya zambun fa akhaafu ai yaqtuloon

1.arabic: قال كلا ۖ فاذهبا بآياتنا ۖ إنا معكم مستمعون
1.english: [Allah] said, "No. Go both of you with Our signs; indeed, We are with you, listening.
1.transliteration: Qaala kallaa fazhabaa bi Aayaatinaaa innaa ma'akum mustami'oon

1.arabic: فأتيا فرعون فقولا إنا رسول رب العالمين
1.english: Go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the messengers of the Lord of the worlds,
1.transliteration: Faatiyaa Fir'awna faqoolaaa innaa Rasoolu Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: [Commanded to say], "Send with us the Children of Israel."'"
1.transliteration: An arsil ma'anaa Baneee Israaa'eel

1.arabic: قال ألم نربك فينا وليدا ولبثت فينا من عمرك سنين
1.english: [Pharaoh] said, "Did we not raise you among us as a child, and you remained among us for years of your life?
1.transliteration: Qaala alam nurabbika feenaa waleedanw wa labista feenaa min 'umurika sineen

1.arabic: وفعلت فعلتك التي فعلت وأنت من الكافرين
1.english: And [then] you did your deed which you did, and you were of the ungrateful."
1.transliteration: Wa fa'alta fa'latakal latee fa'alta wa anta minal kaafireen

1.arabic: قال فعلتها إذا وأنا من الضالين
1.english: [Moses] said, "I did it, then, while I was of those astray.
1.transliteration: Qaala fa'altuhaaa izanw wa ana minad daaaleen

1.arabic: ففررت منكم لما خفتكم فوهب لي ربي حكما وجعلني من المرسلين
1.english: So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me wisdom and prophethood and appointed me [as one] of the messengers.
1.transliteration: Fafarartu minkum lam maa khiftukum fawahaba lee Rabbee hukmanw wa ja'alanee minal mursaleen

1.arabic: وتلك نعمة تمنها علي أن عبدت بني إسرائيل
1.english: And is this a favor of which you remind me - that you have enslaved the Children of Israel?"
1.transliteration: Wa tilka ni'matun tamun nuhaa 'alaiya an 'abbatta Baneee Israaa'eel

1.english: Said Pharaoh, "And what is the Lord of the worlds?"
1.transliteration: Qaala Fir'awnu wa maa Rabbul 'aalameen

1.arabic: قال رب السماوات والأرض وما بينهما ۖ إن كنتم موقنين
1.english: [Moses] said, "The Lord of the heavens and earth and that between them, if you should be convinced."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbus samaawaati wal ardi wa maa bainahumaa in kuntum mooqineen

1.english: [Pharaoh] said to those around him, "Do you not hear?"
1.transliteration: Qaala liman hawlahooo alaa tastami'oon

1.english: [Moses] said, "Your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbukum wa Rabbu aabaaa'ikumul awwaleen

1.arabic: قال إن رسولكم الذي أرسل إليكم لمجنون
1.english: [Pharaoh] said, "Indeed, your 'messenger' who has been sent to you is mad."
1.transliteration: Qaala inna Rasoolakumul lazee ursila ilaikum lamajnoon

1.arabic: قال رب المشرق والمغرب وما بينهما ۖ إن كنتم تعقلون
1.english: [Moses] said, "Lord of the east and the west and that between them, if you were to reason."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbul mashriqi wal maghribi wa maa baina humaa in kuntum ta'qiloon

1.arabic: قال لئن اتخذت إلها غيري لأجعلنك من المسجونين
1.english: [Pharaoh] said, "If you take a god other than me, I will surely place you among those imprisoned."
1.transliteration: Qaala la'init takhazta ilaahan ghairee la aj'alannaka minal masjooneen

1.english: [Moses] said, "Even if I brought you proof manifest?"
1.transliteration: Qaala awalw ji'tuka bishai'im mubeen

1.arabic: قال فأت به إن كنت من الصادقين
1.english: [Pharaoh] said, "Then bring it, if you should be of the truthful."
1.transliteration: Qaala faati biheee in kunta minas saadiqeen

1.arabic: فألقى عصاه فإذا هي ثعبان مبين
1.english: So [Moses] threw his staff, and suddenly it was a serpent manifest.
1.transliteration: Fa alqaa 'asaahu fa izaaa hiya su'baanum mubeen

1.arabic: ونزع يده فإذا هي بيضاء للناظرين
1.english: And he drew out his hand; thereupon it was white for the observers.
1.transliteration: Wa naza'a yadahoo faizaa hiya baidaaa'u linnaa zireen

1.arabic: قال للملإ حوله إن هذا لساحر عليم
1.english: [Pharaoh] said to the eminent ones around him, "Indeed, this is a learned magician.
1.transliteration: Qaala lilmala-i hawlahooo inna haazaa lasaahirun 'aleem

1.arabic: يريد أن يخرجكم من أرضكم بسحره فماذا تأمرون
1.english: He wants to drive you out of your land by his magic, so what do you advise?"
1.transliteration: Yureedu ai yukhrijakum min ardikum bisihrihee famaazaa taamuroon

1.arabic: قالوا أرجه وأخاه وابعث في المدائن حاشرين
1.english: They said, "Postpone [the matter of] him and his brother and send among the cities gatherers
1.transliteration: Qaalooo arjih wa akhaahu wab'as filmadaaa'ini haashireen

1.english: Who will bring you every learned, skilled magician."
1.transliteration: Yaatooka bikulli sah haarin 'aleem

1.english: So the magicians were assembled for the appointment of a well-known day.
1.transliteration: Fa jumi'as saharatu limeeqaati Yawmim ma'loom

1.english: And it was said to the people, "Will you congregate
1.transliteration: Wa qeela linnaasi hal antum mujtami'oon

1.arabic: لعلنا نتبع السحرة إن كانوا هم الغالبين
1.english: That we might follow the magicians if they are the predominant?"
1.transliteration: La'allanaa nattabi'us saharata in kaanoo humul ghaalibeen

1.arabic: فلما جاء السحرة قالوا لفرعون أئن لنا لأجرا إن كنا نحن الغالبين
1.english: And when the magicians arrived, they said to Pharaoh, "Is there indeed for us a reward if we are the predominant?"
1.transliteration: Falammaa jaaa'as saharatu qaaloo li Fir'awna a'inna lanaa la ajjran in kunnaa nahnul ghaalibeen

1.arabic: قال نعم وإنكم إذا لمن المقربين
1.english: He said, "Yes, and indeed, you will then be of those near [to me]."
1.transliteration: Qaala na'am wa innakum izal laminal muqarrabeen

1.arabic: قال لهم موسى ألقوا ما أنتم ملقون
1.english: Moses said to them, "Throw whatever you will throw."
1.transliteration: Qaala lahum Moosaaa alqoo maaa antum mulqoon

1.arabic: فألقوا حبالهم وعصيهم وقالوا بعزة فرعون إنا لنحن الغالبون
1.english: So they threw their ropes and their staffs and said, "By the might of Pharaoh, indeed it is we who are predominant."
1.transliteration: Fa alqaw hibaalahum wa 'isiyyahum wa qaaloo bi'izzati Fir'awna innaa lanahnul ghaaliboon

1.arabic: فألقى موسى عصاه فإذا هي تلقف ما يأفكون
1.english: Then Moses threw his staff, and at once it devoured what they falsified.
1.transliteration: Fa alqaa Moosaa 'asaahu fa izaa hiya talqafu maa yaafikoon

1.english: So the magicians fell down in prostration [to Allah].
1.transliteration: Fa ulqiyas saharatu saajideen

1.english: They said, "We have believed in the Lord of the worlds,
1.transliteration: Qaalooo aamannaa bi Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: The Lord of Moses and Aaron."
1.transliteration: Rabbi Moosaa wa Haaroon

1.arabic: قال آمنتم له قبل أن آذن لكم ۖ إنه لكبيركم الذي علمكم السحر فلسوف تعلمون ۚ لأقطعن أيديكم وأرجلكم من خلاف ولأصلبنكم أجمعين
1.english: [Pharaoh] said, "You believed Moses before I gave you permission. Indeed, he is your leader who has taught you magic, but you are going to know. I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all."
1.transliteration: Qaala aamantum lahoo qabla an aazana lakum innahoo lakabeerukumul lazee 'alla makumus sihra falasawfa ta'lamoon; la uqatti'anna aidiyakum wa arjulakum min khilaafinw wa la usallibanna kum ajma'een

1.arabic: قالوا لا ضير ۖ إنا إلى ربنا منقلبون
1.english: They said, "No harm. Indeed, to our Lord we will return.
1.transliteration: Qaaloo la daira innaaa ilaa Rabbinaa munqalliboon

1.arabic: إنا نطمع أن يغفر لنا ربنا خطايانا أن كنا أول المؤمنين
1.english: Indeed, we aspire that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers."
1.transliteration: Innaa natma'u ai yaghfira lanaa Rabbunna khataa yaanaaa an kunnaaa awwalal mu'mineen

1.arabic: ۞ وأوحينا إلى موسى أن أسر بعبادي إنكم متبعون
1.english: And We inspired to Moses, "Travel by night with My servants; indeed, you will be pursued."
1.transliteration: Wa awhainaaa ilaa Moosaaa an asri bi'ibaadeee innakum muttaba'oon

1.english: Then Pharaoh sent among the cities gatherers
1.transliteration: Fa arsala Fir'awnu filmadaaa'ini haashireen

1.english: [And said], "Indeed, those are but a small band,
1.transliteration: Inna haaa'ulaaa'i lashir zimatun qaleeloon

1.english: And indeed, they are enraging us,
1.transliteration: Wa innahum lanaa laghaaa'izoon

1.english: And indeed, we are a cautious society... "
1.transliteration: Wa innaa lajamee'un haaziroon

1.english: So We removed them from gardens and springs
1.transliteration: Fa akhrajnaahum min Jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.english: And treasures and honorable station -
1.transliteration: Wa kunoozinw wa ma qaamin kareem

1.english: Thus. And We caused to inherit it the Children of Israel.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika wa awrasnaahaa Baneee Israaa'eel

1.english: So they pursued them at sunrise.
1.transliteration: Fa atba'oohum mushriqeen

1.arabic: فلما تراءى الجمعان قال أصحاب موسى إنا لمدركون
1.english: And when the two companies saw one another, the companions of Moses said, "Indeed, we are to be overtaken!"
1.transliteration: Falammaa taraaa'al jam'aani qaala as haabu Moosaaa innaa lamudrakoon

1.arabic: قال كلا ۖ إن معي ربي سيهدين
1.english: [Moses] said, "No! Indeed, with me is my Lord; He will guide me."
1.transliteration: Qaala kallaaa inna ma'iya Rabbee sa yahdeen

1.arabic: فأوحينا إلى موسى أن اضرب بعصاك البحر ۖ فانفلق فكان كل فرق كالطود العظيم
1.english: Then We inspired to Moses, "Strike with your staff the sea," and it parted, and each portion was like a great towering mountain.
1.transliteration: Fa awhainaaa ilaa Moosaaa anidrib bi'asaakal bahra fanfalaqa fakaana kullu firqin kattawdil 'azeem

1.english: And We advanced thereto the pursuers.
1.transliteration: Wa azlafnaa sammal aakhareen

1.english: And We saved Moses and those with him, all together.
1.transliteration: Wa anjainaa Moosaa wa mam ma'ahooo ajma'een

1.english: Then We drowned the others.
1.transliteration: Summa aghraqnal aakhareen

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayaah; wa maa kaana aksaru hu mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: And recite to them the news of Abraham,
1.transliteration: Watlu 'alaihim naba-a Ibraaheem

1.arabic: إذ قال لأبيه وقومه ما تعبدون
1.english: When he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?"
1.transliteration: Iz qaala li abeehi wa qawmihee maa ta'budoon

1.arabic: قالوا نعبد أصناما فنظل لها عاكفين
1.english: They said, "We worship idols and remain to them devoted."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo na'budu asnaaman fanazallu lahaa 'aakifeen

1.english: He said, "Do they hear you when you supplicate?
1.transliteration: Qaala hal yasma'oona kum iz tad'oon

1.english: Or do they benefit you, or do they harm?"
1.transliteration: Aw yanfa'oonakum aw yadurroon

1.arabic: قالوا بل وجدنا آباءنا كذلك يفعلون
1.english: They said, "But we found our fathers doing thus."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo bal wajadnaaa aabaaa 'anaa kazaalika yaf'aloon

1.english: He said, "Then do you see what you have been worshipping,
1.transliteration: Qaala afara 'aitum maa kuntum ta'budoon

1.english: You and your ancient forefathers?
1.transliteration: Antum wa aabaaa'ukumul aqdamoon

1.arabic: فإنهم عدو لي إلا رب العالمين
1.english: Indeed, they are enemies to me, except the Lord of the worlds,
1.transliteration: Fa innahum 'aduwwwul leee illaa Rabbal 'aalameen

1.english: Who created me, and He [it is who] guides me.
1.transliteration: Allazee khalaqanee fa Huwa yahdeen

1.english: And it is He who feeds me and gives me drink.
1.transliteration: Wallazee Huwa yut'imunee wa yasqeen

1.english: And when I am ill, it is He who cures me
1.transliteration: Wa izaa mardtu fahuwa yashfeen

1.english: And who will cause me to die and then bring me to life
1.transliteration: Wallazee yumeetunee summa yuhyeen

1.arabic: والذي أطمع أن يغفر لي خطيئتي يوم الدين
1.english: And who I aspire that He will forgive me my sin on the Day of Recompense."
1.transliteration: Wallazeee atma'u ai yaghfira lee khateee' atee Yawmad Deen

1.arabic: رب هب لي حكما وألحقني بالصالحين
1.english: [And he said], "My Lord, grant me authority and join me with the righteous.
1.transliteration: Rabbi hab lee hukmanw wa alhiqnee bis saaliheen

1.arabic: واجعل لي لسان صدق في الآخرين
1.english: And grant me a reputation of honor among later generations.
1.transliteration: Waj'al lee lisaana sidqin fil aakhireen

1.english: And place me among the inheritors of the Garden of Pleasure.
1.transliteration: Waj'alnee minw warasati Jannnatin Na'eem

1.arabic: واغفر لأبي إنه كان من الضالين
1.english: And forgive my father. Indeed, he has been of those astray.
1.transliteration: Waghfir li abeee innahoo kaana mind daalleen

1.english: And do not disgrace me on the Day they are [all] resurrected -
1.transliteration: Wa laa tukhzinee Yawma yub'asoon

1.arabic: يوم لا ينفع مال ولا بنون
1.english: The Day when there will not benefit [anyone] wealth or children
1.transliteration: Yawma laa yanfa'u maalunw wa laa banoon

1.arabic: إلا من أتى الله بقلب سليم
1.english: But only one who comes to Allah with a sound heart."
1.transliteration: Illaa man atal laaha biqalbin saleem

1.english: And Paradise will be brought near [that Day] to the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa uzlifatil Jannatu lilmuttaqeen

1.english: And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,
1.transliteration: Wa burrizatil Jaheemu lilghaaween

1.arabic: وقيل لهم أين ما كنتم تعبدون
1.english: And it will be said to them, "Where are those you used to worship
1.transliteration: Wa qeela lahum aina maa kuntum ta'budoon

1.arabic: من دون الله هل ينصرونكم أو ينتصرون
1.english: Other than Allah? Can they help you or help themselves?"
1.transliteration: Min doonil laahi hal yansuroonakum aw yantasiroon

1.english: So they will be overturned into Hellfire, they and the deviators
1.transliteration: Fakubkiboo feehaa hum walghaawoon

1.english: And the soldiers of Iblees, all together.
1.transliteration: Wa junoodu Ibleesa ajma'oon

1.english: They will say while they dispute therein,
1.transliteration: Qaaloo wa hum feehaa yakkhtasimoon

1.arabic: تالله إن كنا لفي ضلال مبين
1.english: "By Allah, we were indeed in manifest error
1.transliteration: Tallaahi in kunnaa lafee dalaalim mubeen

1.english: When we equated you with the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Iz nusawweekum bi Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: And no one misguided us except the criminals.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa adallanaaa illal mujrimoon

1.english: So now we have no intercessors
1.transliteration: Famaa lanaa min shaa fi'een

1.english: And not a devoted friend.
1.transliteration: Wa laa sadeeqin hameem

1.arabic: فلو أن لنا كرة فنكون من المؤمنين
1.english: Then if we only had a return [to the world] and could be of the believers... "
1.transliteration: Falaw anna lanaa karratan fanakoona minal mu'mineen

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayatanw wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: The people of Noah denied the messengers
1.transliteration: Kazzabat qawmu Noohinil Mursaleen

1.arabic: إذ قال لهم أخوهم نوح ألا تتقون
1.english: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Noohun alaa tattaqoon

1.english: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
1.transliteration: Innee lakum Rasoolun ameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqullaaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: وما أسألكم عليه من أجر ۖ إن أجري إلا على رب العالمين
1.english: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me."
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.english: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"
1.transliteration: Qaalooo anu'minu laka wattaba 'akal arzaloon

1.arabic: قال وما علمي بما كانوا يعملون
1.english: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?
1.transliteration: Qaala wa maa 'ilmee bimaa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: إن حسابهم إلا على ربي ۖ لو تشعرون
1.english: Their account is only upon my Lord, if you [could] perceive.
1.transliteration: In hisaabuhum illaa 'alaa Rabbee law tash'uroon

1.english: And I am not one to drive away the believers.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa ana bitaaridil mu'mineen

1.english: I am only a clear warner."
1.transliteration: In ana illaa nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: قالوا لئن لم تنته يا نوح لتكونن من المرجومين
1.english: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo la'il lam tantahi yaa Noohu latakoonanna minal marjoomeen

1.english: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbi inna qawmee kazzaboon

1.arabic: فافتح بيني وبينهم فتحا ونجني ومن معي من المؤمنين
1.english: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."
1.transliteration: Faftab bainee wa bai nahum fat hanw wa najjinee wa mam ma'iya minal mu'mineen

1.arabic: فأنجيناه ومن معه في الفلك المشحون
1.english: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.
1.transliteration: Fa anjainaahu wa mamma'ahoo fil fulkil mashhoon

1.english: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.
1.transliteration: Summa aghraqnaa ba'dul baaqeen

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayaah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: 'Aad denied the messengers
1.transliteration: Kazzabat 'Aadunil mursaleen

1.arabic: إذ قال لهم أخوهم هود ألا تتقون
1.english: When their brother Hud said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Hoodun alaa tattaqoon

1.english: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
1.transliteration: Innee lakum Rasoolun ameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqullaaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: وما أسألكم عليه من أجر ۖ إن أجري إلا على رب العالمين
1.english: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: Do you construct on every elevation a sign, amusing yourselves,
1.transliteration: Atabnoona bikulli ree'in aayatan ta'basoon

1.english: And take for yourselves palaces and fortresses that you might abide eternally?
1.transliteration: Wa tattakhizoona masaani'a la'allakum takhludoon

1.english: And when you strike, you strike as tyrants.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa batashtum batashtum jabbaareen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.english: And fear He who provided you with that which you know,
1.transliteration: Wattaqul lazeee amad dakum bimaa ta'lamoon

1.english: Provided you with grazing livestock and children
1.transliteration: Amaddakum bi an'aa minw wa baneen

1.english: And gardens and springs.
1.transliteration: Wa jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.arabic: إني أخاف عليكم عذاب يوم عظيم
1.english: Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day."
1.transliteration: Innee akhaafu 'alaikum 'azaaba Yawmin 'azeem

1.arabic: قالوا سواء علينا أوعظت أم لم تكن من الواعظين
1.english: They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not of the advisors.
1.transliteration: Qaaloo sawaaa'un 'alainaaa awa 'azta am lam takum minal waa'izeen

1.english: This is not but the custom of the former peoples,
1.transliteration: In haazaaa illaa khuluqul awwaleen

1.english: And we are not to be punished."
1.transliteration: Wa maa nahnu bimu 'azzabeen

1.arabic: فكذبوه فأهلكناهم ۗ إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: And they denied him, so We destroyed them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Fakazzaboohu fa ahlaknaahum; inna fee zaalika la aayah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: Thamud denied the messengers
1.transliteration: Kazzabat Samoodul mursaleen

1.arabic: إذ قال لهم أخوهم صالح ألا تتقون
1.english: When their brother Salih said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Saalihun alaa tattaqoon

1.english: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
1.transliteration: Innee lakum Rasoolun ameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: وما أسألكم عليه من أجر ۖ إن أجري إلا على رب العالمين
1.english: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: Will you be left in what is here, secure [from death],
1.transliteration: Atutrakoona fee maa haahunnaaa aamineen

1.english: Within gardens and springs
1.transliteration: Fee jannaatinw wa 'uyoon

1.english: And fields of crops and palm trees with softened fruit?
1.transliteration: Wa zuroo inw wa nakhlin tal 'uhaa hadeem

1.english: And you carve out of the mountains, homes, with skill.
1.transliteration: Wa tanhitoona minal jibaali buyootan faariheen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.english: And do not obey the order of the transgressors,
1.transliteration: Wa laa tutee'ooo amral musrifeen

1.arabic: الذين يفسدون في الأرض ولا يصلحون
1.english: Who cause corruption in the land and do not amend."
1.transliteration: Allazeena yufsidoona fil ardi wa laa yuslihoon

1.english: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
1.transliteration: Qaalooo innamaa anta minal musahhareen

1.arabic: ما أنت إلا بشر مثلنا فأت بآية إن كنت من الصادقين
1.english: You are but a man like ourselves, so bring a sign, if you should be of the truthful."
1.transliteration: Maaa anta illaa basharum mislunaa faati bi Aayatin in kunta minas saadiqeen

1.arabic: قال هذه ناقة لها شرب ولكم شرب يوم معلوم
1.english: He said, "This is a she-camel. For her is a [time of] drink, and for you is a [time of] drink, [each] on a known day.
1.transliteration: Qaala haazihee naaqatul lahaa shirbunw w alakum shirbu yawmim ma'loom

1.arabic: ولا تمسوها بسوء فيأخذكم عذاب يوم عظيم
1.english: And do not touch her with harm, lest you be seized by the punishment of a terrible day."
1.transliteration: Wa laa tamassoohaa bisooo'in fa yaakhuzakum 'azaabu Yawmin 'Azeem

1.english: But they hamstrung her and so became regretful.
1.transliteration: Fa'aqaroohaa fa asbahoo naadimeen

1.arabic: فأخذهم العذاب ۗ إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: And the punishment seized them. Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Fa akhazahumul 'azaab; inna fee zaalika la Aayah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum m'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: The people of Lot denied the messengers
1.transliteration: kazzabat qawmu Lootinil mursaleen

1.arabic: إذ قال لهم أخوهم لوط ألا تتقون
1.english: When their brother Lot said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala lahum akhoohum Lootun alaa tattaqoon

1.english: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
1.transliteration: Innee lakum rasoolun ameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: وما أسألكم عليه من أجر ۖ إن أجري إلا على رب العالمين
1.english: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: Do you approach males among the worlds
1.transliteration: Ataatoonaz zukraana minal 'aalameen

1.arabic: وتذرون ما خلق لكم ربكم من أزواجكم ۚ بل أنتم قوم عادون
1.english: And leave what your Lord has created for you as mates? But you are a people transgressing."
1.transliteration: Wa tazaroona maa khalaqa lakum Rabbukum min azwaajikum; bal antum qawmun 'aadoon

1.arabic: قالوا لئن لم تنته يا لوط لتكونن من المخرجين
1.english: They said, "If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be of those evicted."
1.transliteration: Qaloo la'il lam tantahi yaa Lootu latakoonanna minal mukhrajeen

1.english: He said, "Indeed, I am, toward your deed, of those who detest [it].
1.transliteration: Qaala innee li'amalikum minal qaaleen

1.english: My Lord, save me and my family from [the consequence of] what they do."
1.transliteration: Rabbi najjjinee wa ahlee mimmmaa ya'maloon

1.english: So We saved him and his family, all,
1.transliteration: Fanajjainaahu wa ahlahooo ajma'een

1.english: Except an old woman among those who remained behind.
1.transliteration: Illaa 'ajoozan filghaabireen

1.english: Then We destroyed the others.
1.transliteration: Summa dammarnal aa khareen

1.arabic: وأمطرنا عليهم مطرا ۖ فساء مطر المنذرين
1.english: And We rained upon them a rain [of stones], and evil was the rain of those who were warned.
1.transliteration: Wa amtarnaa 'alaihim mataran fasaaa'a matarul munzareen

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la Huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: The companions of the thicket denied the messengers
1.transliteration: Kazzaba As haabul Aykatil mursaleen

1.arabic: إذ قال لهم شعيب ألا تتقون
1.english: When Shu'ayb said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?
1.transliteration: Iz qaala lahum Shu'aybun alaa tattaqoon

1.english: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.
1.transliteration: Innee lakum Rasoolun ameen

1.english: So fear Allah and obey me.
1.transliteration: Fattaqul laaha wa atee'oon

1.arabic: وما أسألكم عليه من أجر ۖ إن أجري إلا على رب العالمين
1.english: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa as'alukum 'alaihi min ajrin in ajriya illaa 'alaa Rabbil 'aalameen

1.arabic: ۞ أوفوا الكيل ولا تكونوا من المخسرين
1.english: Give full measure and do not be of those who cause loss.
1.transliteration: Awful kaila wa laa takoonoo minal mukhsireen

1.english: And weigh with an even balance.
1.transliteration: Wa zinoo bilqistaasil mustaqeem

1.arabic: ولا تبخسوا الناس أشياءهم ولا تعثوا في الأرض مفسدين
1.english: And do not deprive people of their due and do not commit abuse on earth, spreading corruption.
1.transliteration: Wa laa tabkhasun naasa ashyaaa 'ahum wa laa ta'saw fil ardi mufsideen

1.english: And fear He who created you and the former creation."
1.transliteration: Wattaqul lazee khalaqakum waljibillatal awwaleen

1.english: They said, "You are only of those affected by magic.
1.transliteration: Qaalooo innamaa anta minal musahhareen

1.arabic: وما أنت إلا بشر مثلنا وإن نظنك لمن الكاذبين
1.english: You are but a man like ourselves, and indeed, we think you are among the liars.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa anta illaa basharum mislunaa wa innazunnuka laminal kaazibeen

1.arabic: فأسقط علينا كسفا من السماء إن كنت من الصادقين
1.english: So cause to fall upon us fragments of the sky, if you should be of the truthful."
1.transliteration: Fa asqit 'alainaa kisafam minas samaaa'i in kunta minas saadiqeen

1.english: He said, "My Lord is most knowing of what you do."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbeee a'lamu bimaa ta'maloon

1.arabic: فكذبوه فأخذهم عذاب يوم الظلة ۚ إنه كان عذاب يوم عظيم
1.english: And they denied him, so the punishment of the day of the black cloud seized them. Indeed, it was the punishment of a terrible day.
1.transliteration: Fakazzaboohu fa akhazahum 'azaabu Yawmiz zullah; innahoo kaana 'azaaba Yawmin 'Azeem

1.arabic: إن في ذلك لآية ۖ وما كان أكثرهم مؤمنين
1.english: Indeed in that is a sign, but most of them were not to be believers.
1.transliteration: Inna fee zaalika la Aayah; wa maa kaana aksaruhum mu'mineen

1.english: And indeed, your Lord - He is the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.
1.transliteration: Wa inna Rabbaka la huwal 'Azeezur Raheem

1.english: And indeed, the Qur'an is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo latanzeelu Rabbil 'aalameen

1.english: The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down
1.transliteration: Nazala bihir Roohul Ameen

1.english: Upon your heart, [O Muhammad] - that you may be of the warners -
1.transliteration: 'Alaa qalbika litakoona minal munzireen

1.english: In a clear Arabic language.
1.transliteration: Bilisaanin 'Arabiyyim mubeen

1.english: And indeed, it is [mentioned] in the scriptures of former peoples.
1.transliteration: Wa innahoo lafee Zuburil awwaleen

1.arabic: أولم يكن لهم آية أن يعلمه علماء بني إسرائيل
1.english: And has it not been a sign to them that it is recognized by the scholars of the Children of Israel?
1.transliteration: Awalam yakul lahum Aayatan ai ya'lamahoo 'ulamaaa'u Baneee Israaa'eel

1.english: And even if We had revealed it to one among the foreigners
1.transliteration: Wa law nazzalnaahu 'alaa ba'dil a'jameen

1.arabic: فقرأه عليهم ما كانوا به مؤمنين
1.english: And he had recited it to them [perfectly], they would [still] not have been believers in it.
1.transliteration: Faqara ahoo 'alaihim maa kaanoo bihee mu'mineen

1.english: Thus have We inserted disbelief into the hearts of the criminals.
1.transliteration: Kazaalika salaknaahu fee quloobil mujrimeen

1.arabic: لا يؤمنون به حتى يروا العذاب الأليم
1.english: They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment.
1.transliteration: Laa yu'minoona bihee hattaa yarawul 'azaabal aleem

1.english: And it will come to them suddenly while they perceive [it] not.
1.transliteration: Fayaatiyahum baghtatanw wa hum laa yash'uroon

1.english: And they will say, "May we be reprieved?"
1.transliteration: Fa yaqooloo hal nahnu munzaroon

1.english: So for Our punishment are they impatient?
1.transliteration: Aafabi 'azaabinaa yasta'jiloon

1.english: Then have you considered if We gave them enjoyment for years
1.transliteration: Aara'aita im matta'naahum sineen

1.english: And then there came to them that which they were promised?
1.transliteration: Summa jaaa'ahum maa kaanoo yoo'adoon

1.arabic: ما أغنى عنهم ما كانوا يمتعون
1.english: They would not be availed by the enjoyment with which they were provided.
1.transliteration: Maaa aghnaaa 'anhum maa kaanoo yumaatoo'oon

1.arabic: وما أهلكنا من قرية إلا لها منذرون
1.english: And We did not destroy any city except that it had warners
1.transliteration: Wa maaa ahlaknaa min qaryatin illaa lahaa munziroon

1.english: As a reminder; and never have We been unjust.
1.transliteration: Zikraa wa maa kunnaa zaalimeen

1.english: And the devils have not brought the revelation down.
1.transliteration: Wa maa tanazzalat bihish Shayaateen

1.english: It is not allowable for them, nor would they be able.
1.transliteration: Wa maa yambaghee lahum wa maa yastatee'oon

1.english: Indeed they, from [its] hearing, are removed.
1.transliteration: Innahum 'anis sam'i lama'zooloon

1.arabic: فلا تدع مع الله إلها آخر فتكون من المعذبين
1.english: So do not invoke with Allah another deity and [thus] be among the punished.
1.transliteration: Falaa tad'u ma'al laahi ilaahan aakhara fatakoona minal mu'azzabeen

1.english: And warn, [O Muhammad], your closest kindred.
1.transliteration: Wa anzir 'asheeratakal aqrabeen

1.arabic: واخفض جناحك لمن اتبعك من المؤمنين
1.english: And lower your wing to those who follow you of the believers.
1.transliteration: Wakhfid janaahaka limanit taba 'aka minal mu'mineen

1.arabic: فإن عصوك فقل إني بريء مما تعملون
1.english: And if they disobey you, then say, "Indeed, I am disassociated from what you are doing."
1.transliteration: Fa in asawka faqul innee bareee'um mimmmaa ta'maloon

1.english: And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful,
1.transliteration: Wa tawakkal alal 'Azeezir Raheem

1.english: Who sees you when you arise
1.transliteration: Allazee yaraaka heena taqoom

1.english: And your movement among those who prostrate.
1.transliteration: Wa taqallubaka fis saajideen

1.english: Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Innahoo Huwas Samee'ul 'Aleem

1.arabic: هل أنبئكم على من تنزل الشياطين
1.english: Shall I inform you upon whom the devils descend?
1.transliteration: Hal unabbi'ukum 'alaa man tanazzalush Shayaateen

1.english: They descend upon every sinful liar.
1.transliteration: Tanazzalu 'alaa kulli affaakin aseem

1.english: They pass on what is heard, and most of them are liars.
1.transliteration: Yulqoonas sam'a wa aksaruhum aaziboon

1.english: And the poets - [only] the deviators follow them;
1.transliteration: Washshu 'araaa'u yattabi 'uhumul ghaawoon

1.arabic: ألم تر أنهم في كل واد يهيمون
1.english: Do you not see that in every valley they roam
1.transliteration: Alam tara annahum fee kulli waadiny yaheemoon

1.english: And that they say what they do not do? -
1.transliteration: Wa annahum yaqooloona ma laa yaf'aloon

1.arabic: إلا الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات وذكروا الله كثيرا وانتصروا من بعد ما ظلموا ۗ وسيعلم الذين ظلموا أي منقلب ينقلبون
1.english: Except those [poets] who believe and do righteous deeds and remember Allah often and defend [the Muslims] after they were wronged. And those who have wronged are going to know to what [kind of] return they will be returned.
1.transliteration: Illal lazeena aamanoo w a'amilus saalihaati wa zakarul laaha kaseeranw wantasaroo mim ba'di maa zulimoo; wa saya'lamul lazeena zalamooo aiya munqalbiny yanqaliboon
